preFix.title: Macramé rainbow wall hanging
preFix.subtitle: A semicircular rainbow in five colours of 5 mm cotton cord on a wooden dowel, approximately 35 cm wide
preFix.excerpt: A semicircular macramé rainbow worked in five colours of 5 mm cotton cord, each colour forming one arc of the rainbow worked in square knots. All arcs are mounted on a wooden dowel with lark's head knots. White fringe at each end of the dowel represents the cloud tassels. Finished size: approximately 35 cm wide at the base.
preFix.sourceNotes: Observation-led from the mid-20th-century macramé revival. Rainbow-shaped wall hangings are a widely made pattern type in the contemporary macramé revival. Square knots and lark's head mounts are documented in Graumont and Hensel, Encyclopedia of Knots and Fancy Rope Work, Cornell Maritime Press, 1939.

content.text: The macramé rainbow is made as five separate colour arcs, each mounted on the same wooden dowel with

. The arcs are built in order from the innermost (shortest) to the outermost (longest). Each arc hangs at a different length from the dowel to create the curved rainbow shape. White cotton fringe is mounted at each end of the dowel and brushed out into the cloud tassels.

Use 5 mm cotton cord in 5 colours. The five arcs from innermost to outermost are: red (or deep pink), orange, yellow, green, and blue (or purple for the outer arc). Each arc uses a different number of cords because the outer arcs span a wider section of the dowel.
Arc 1 (innermost, red): 6 cords at 60 cm each
Arc 5 (outermost, blue or purple): 14 cords at 140 cm each
Cloud fringe (white): 16 cords at 30 cm each (8 per side)

definition.caption: Square knot — two working cords around two filler cords; worked in rows to fill each coloured arc band

content.text: Mark the dowel to show where each arc will be mounted. The innermost arc occupies the central 10 cm of the dowel; each successive arc adds approximately 3 to 4 cm on each side. Leave 5 to 6 cm at each outer end of the dowel for the cloud fringe. Secure the dowel to your macramé board at working height.

Mount the 6 red cords in the central section of the dowel using lark's head knots, giving 12 working ends. Tie 3 square knots across (groups of 4 cords). Drop down approximately 2 cm and work a second row of alternating square knots: skip the first 2 cords, tie 2 square knots across, leave the last 2 cords. Add one more square knot in the centre below. Trim the fringe ends at an angle or in a V to give the arc shape at the bottom.
Step 3: Mount and work Arcs 2 to 5

Work each subsequent arc in the same way, mounting slightly wider on the dowel and using progressively more cords. Each arc is mounted so that its knotted section hangs slightly lower than the arc inside it: this is what creates the curved rainbow silhouette. Arc 5 (outermost) should hang at least 5 to 6 cm lower than Arc 1 at the lowest point. Trim each arc's fringe in a gentle curve.

Mount 8 white cords on each outer end of the dowel with lark's head knots, giving 16 working ends per side. Leave them as loose fringe without knotting. Use the fringe comb to brush out each cord end thoroughly until the cotton fibres separate into a fluffy cloud texture. Trim the fringe in a rounded shape to suggest a full cloud volume.

Weave any loose knot tails back into each arc from behind using a blunt darning needle and trim flush. Check that all arcs hang in clean curves without cords crossing between colours. The piece hangs from the dowel using a short length of cord looped around each end.

tricolon: tricolon (three parallel items) — two adjectives almost always beats three
